entry.title: SAC Compass - any instructions available?
entry.content:
Hi community,

its great that compass is now available in our SAC Test Tenant, I am looking forward to using it!

Is there any instructional material available at this point? I havent found any on SAC Help Portal, SAC Community, SAP Youtube or directly in SAC.

Please let me know if there is any!

Kind regards,

Eric

entry.title: Introducing Joule: SAP’s AI Copilot for Seamless Business Interactions
entry.content:
Introducing Joule: SAP’s AI Copilot for Seamless Business Interactions

 

SAP is transforming the way businesses interact with their systems with Joule, its AI-powered copilot designed to enhance productivity and simplify tasks. Joule serves as a centralized AI assistant across the SAP ecosystem, providing a seamless and context-aware user experience. 

What Makes Joule Unique?

One AI Copilot for SAP: Joule integrates across SAP cloud solutions like SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, allowing users to interact naturally through language.

Out-of-the-Box & Native Integration: With pre-configured content, Joule is ready to use with minimal setup, making it instantly valuable.

Consistent User Experience: Whether accessed via SAP Start, mobile, or application UIs, Joule ensures a smooth and familiar interaction.

Reliable & Context-Aware: Joule understands user roles, permissions, and the SAP products they use, ensuring relevant responses. 

Responsible & Secure: With strict data protection policies, Joule does not use customer data for AI training or third-party purposes.

 

How Does Joule Help?

  • Provides context-aware responses based on business transactions and data.
  • Maintains cross-application reasoning for efficient navigation across SAP solutions.
  • Leverages Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for document-grounded responses.
  • Ensures data security & compliance by inheriting user authentication rights.
  • Includes ethical AI guardrails to prevent bias, hate speech, and misuse.

By integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) with SAP’s rich business context, Joule enables employees to work 80% more efficiently. It empowers businesses with faster decision-making, streamlined workflows, and enhanced user interactions—truly revolutionizing the SAP experience.

Joule Interaction Patterns is the main starting point.

It branches into four types of interactions:

  • Transactional: Supports CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations.
  • Navigational: Helps users find relevant SAP screens.
  • Informational: Provides knowledge-based answers using SAP Help and Customer Content (Document Grounding).
  • Analytical: Enables data insights through SAP Analytics Cloud and JustAsk.

This flowchart visually simplifies the interaction patterns of Joule. 

Unlocking Business Knowledge with Joule’s Document Grounding

In today’s workplace, quick access to accurate information is crucial. Joule’s Document Grounding feature ensures employees get the right answers based on company-specific documents—without the need for complex AI training or manual searching.

  • What is Document Grounding?

Document Grounding allows businesses to upload important documents—like HR policies, travel guidelines, and FAQs—into Joule. When employees ask a question, Joule retrieves the most relevant information from these documents and delivers a clear, concise response with source citations.

 

  • Why Does This Matter?

Traditional AI models rely on general knowledge, which may not always reflect company-specific policies. Grounding enhances AI by:

✔️ Providing business-specific answers based on internal documentation.

✔️ Citing sources, increasing transparency and trust.

✔️ Reducing misinformation by pulling directly from company-approved materials.

✔️ Eliminating costly AI retraining, as Joule dynamically retrieves the latest content.

 

  • How It Transforms the Employee Experience?

For instance, in SAP SuccessFactors, employees can ask Joule questions about HR policies and receive instant, reliable answers. This saves time, reduces confusion, and enhances productivity—making company knowledge more accessible to everyone.

entry.title: SAP Build, Joule studio , Beta function not visible on my Build account
entry.content:
Hello All, 

we have activated the paramter "Allow Beta Application/services" on our BTP sub account . 

but in Build we still cant see the option to create custom joule skills. 

http://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-feature/e93aa292-e7f4-449d-9586-f1a8510d5ab6/

Anyone knows how to get this BETA function in our Build ?

entry.title: S4HANA Cloud Trial - Availability of Joule Features
entry.content:
Hi,

I am interested in trying out the SAP Joule features in SAP S4HANA Public Cloud (GROW with SAP). I am willing to take the free trial subscription for this reason. Does anyone know whether the trial version comes pre-activated with Joule features? Thanks in advance for your answer.

entry.title: Joule Custom Term Recognition
entry.content: With SF Joule I saw previously a roadmap item for 'Custom Term Recognition' for H2 2024. I did not see this delivered in H2 2024 is this still coming and when?

entry.title: Joule Extensibility POC for SAP Learning
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
We're considering a proof of concept that involves utilizing the Joule Extensibility feature in relation to the SAP Learning platform (Learn SAP skills | SAP Learning). The idea is to implement a chatbot that enhances user experience by offering improved recommendations and roadmaps based on their search queries. I'm curious about the feasibility of this concept, given that Joule can currently be integrated only with SAP Line of Business products. Is it possible for us to proceed with this use case?

entry.title: SAP Joule use cases in Treasury
entry.content: I'd like to learn about any useful business use cases that leverage Joule to optimise Treasury operations.

entry.title: "Revolutionizing SAP Implementations: How AI is Changing the Game! 🚀"
entry.content:
Leveraging AI in SAP S/4HANA Implementations: A Game Changer?

Introduction

AI is rapidly transforming industries, but how does it fit into SAP S/4HANA implementations? While AI adoption in SAP environments is still low—only 5% of organizations currently use AI and ML—the potential is massive. With SAP Joule introducing 140 AI agents (and planning 400 by 2025), the focus is on improving processes and reducing inefficiencies.

Let's explore how AI can streamline requirement gathering, enhance blueprinting, and optimize the overall SAP implementation process.

 

The Current Landscape: AI in SAP Environments

Organizations still rely on traditional business processes, with SAP use cases focused on data warehousing, self-service analytics, and data discovery. However, integrating SAP data with non-SAP data remains a major challenge for achieving robust AI/ML models. Key barriers include:

  • Limited system access: Incomplete access to source and target systems creates bottlenecks.

  • Software usability issues: High complexity often leads to inefficiencies.

  • Data culture gaps: Organizations need to retrain and hire talent to harness AI’s full potential.

AI can bridge these gaps by enhancing usability and connectivity while reducing the dependency on deep technical expertise.

 

AI in SAP Implementation: Where It Fits

One of the most promising applications of AI in SAP S/4HANA projects is in the requirement gathering and blueprinting phase. Traditionally, this process is time-consuming and involves extensive coordination. AI-powered tools can revolutionize this by:

✅ Capturing and consolidating user requirements automatically
✅ Generating accurate documentation with minimal manual input
✅ Identifying process gaps for testing and training
✅ Reducing misalignment and inefficiencies in design and build phases

AI co-pilots and accelerators are already proving effective in recording user inputs, analyzing data, and structuring blueprints—allowing experts to validate and refine instead of starting from scratch.

 

How AI Streamlines SAP Implementation: A Step-by-Step Flow

1️⃣ AI-Powered Requirement Gathering:
AI captures existing processes and user pain points automatically, reducing reliance on meetings and manual inputs.

2️⃣ Intelligent Blueprinting:
AI-generated documents provide structured insights, identifying key requirements, business rules, and potential process optimizations.

3️⃣ Enhanced Testing & Training:
Gap documents generated by AI facilitate seamless testing, change management, and user training.

4️⃣ Optimized Implementation & Deployment:
With AI-driven documentation, project teams spend less time on redundant tasks, leading to faster go-lives and improved accuracy.

 

Interactive Discussion: AI in SAP Implementations

Q: How can AI-powered tools be used to capture, consolidate, and analyze user requirements and pain points before the design and build phases?

✅ AI accelerators can record user inputs on current processes and requirements, reducing the need for extensive coordination and meetings.
✅ AI consolidates these inputs and generates gap documents, essential for testing and training purposes.
✅ AI-generated documents are reviewed and validated by experts, leading to more accurate and efficient design solutions.
✅ Using AI in this way significantly reduces the time and effort required for requirement gathering and blueprinting, improving implementation quality.

 

Q: How can AI help address challenges in SAP implementations, such as incomplete access to source and target systems, software usability issues, and high complexity?

✅ AI improves ease of use and connectivity, reducing the need for technical skills.
✅ AI co-pilots help bridge the gap between user expectations and SAP reality.
✅ AI enhances connectivity to various source and target systems.
✅ AI improves the ability of technology to meet different use case requirements.

 

Future Outlook: The AI-Driven SAP Revolution

With AI’s role in SAP expanding, organizations that embrace AI-powered accelerators will gain a competitive edge in SAP S/4HANA transformations. While current AI tools focus on process improvement, the future lies in AI’s ability to automate and optimize the entire SAP implementation lifecycle.

💡 What’s Next?

  • More AI-driven automation in testing, training, and deployment

  • Improved integration of SAP and non-SAP data for better AI models

  • Expansion of AI agents in SAP Joule to enhance usability and decision-making

AI in SAP S/4HANA is no longer just an idea—it’s happening now. The question is: Are we ready to leverage it to its full potential?

entry.title: As a SAP MM Functional user, how can I learn the AI part of SAP. What should I learn?
entry.content: I am a SAP MM Functional Consultant. To keep up with the changing dynamics, I want to learn AI with SAP. Any experienced consultant, please provide me a clear roadmap of what to learn and what all resources available both free and paid that I can deep dive into to get a good grasp on AI in SAP. Please assist me.

entry.title: SAP Joule - Identity Provisioning - No assigned Roles displayed
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Over the past few days, we have set up SAP Joule in our Public Cloud environment. This has worked well. But now we have a problem with the assigned roles in identity provisioning. When I search for a user, I get the message that there are now assigned roles for each user.

The problem now is that Joule doesn't show any results when searching for a specific app or anything else because the rules or permissions are missing. Surely the users in the public cloud have the specific roles assigned.

As additional information, we have an IAS and an IPS tenant in Cloud Identity Services.

Has anyone had the same problem so far?

Thanks for your help in advance.

Greetings Tobias

Joule SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP Cloud Identity Services

entry.title: Joule Integration into CAP application
entry.content:
Hello!

I was wondering if I could integrate "Joule as a page" rather than the pop up on clicking the icon into a CAP application that I am working on. Most of the documentations suggest to use Joule Boosters but I can't quite understand it. Any leads would be appreciated.
Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Enable Joule for on-prem ABAP Development
entry.content:
Can Joule be enabled for on-prem ADT ABAP project development for AI Coding Assistance? We are running the newest available version of on-premise S/4 HANA (2023 FSP02 instance). The AI Core set-up guide & Launchpad provisioning guide are a great start but I am missing how the on-premise system enables Joule after the cloud connector is established. 

SAP Note 3513374 mentions the possibility to integrate with SAP_BASIS 756 and up. However, after setting up AI Core and Launchpad - I cannot locate any documentation on how to complete the integration for on-prem systems. 

Our objective is to have Joule enabled from our ADT development environment. Joule is currently not recognized as enabled.

 

The Generative AI in ABAP Cloud Docs only mention validity for Public Cloud & BTP ABAP. Subsequent guides require establishing communication system and communication arrangement configuration, which I do not think that our on-prem system has the ability to configure.

What are the final steps to enable the Joule code assistant in an on-premise S/4 HANA system?

__PRESENT__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

__PRESENT

entry.content: Currently is it possible to connect same Joule instance with S/4HANA Public Cloud and SAP IBP system ?

entry.title: SAP BDC Trial Version Release
entry.content:
Hi All,

Could you help me determine the release date of the trial version of SAP Business Data Cloud? Clients are requesting a demo, and I’d appreciate any insights.

Thanks!

entry.title: Has Joule cross business-role data bleeding been addressed, and how?
entry.content:
With the case of Joule being embedded throughout the core of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public ecosystem, it's natural to assume that Joule has access to all the data in the underlying datastores - which makes sense. However, as with any LLM there substantial considerations regarding bleeding data, either directly or as derivatives. I'm not worried about out-of-organisation security issues, more so cross-organisation security complications.

 

I have been looking around for articles or blog posts regarding Joule's grasp on this / measures in place to prevent bleeding, but I haven't found any.

In the most simple case, imagine two business roles;

- Junior Procurement/Sourcing 

- Senior Procurement/Sourcing

 

The only difference is the junior does not have access to supplier contact information to protect business IP. Given both roles have access to Joule, I would expect outputs of Joule to be governed by similarly to defined access rights in the individual Business Role Access Categories.

In this case, access rights to specific fields are relatively simple, and could essentially be dealt with in the same way as LLM's deal with copyright material / LORA's etc. However, this becomes increasingly complex, and is by no means trivial, as the amount of business roles, data access, data and output types increase.

So:

1. How - technically - does Joule enforce the same minimal-access principles as there exists around RBAC? 

2. Does there exist generalized analytics/monitoring around Joule interactions to identify and act upon data-bleeding instances?

3. Does Joule always work across tenant-data, or is "Joule" an individual agent per. user/union of unique business role combinations scoped to only have access to the data a business role(s) has access to, and is it actively designed to prevent access to more data in the context window?

Unless there exists a formal article on the technical implementation of this, which we can refer to with regards to data sensitivity, it must be assumed that data bleeding is at best possible and at worst not seriously considered in Joule.

entry.title: Joule In Eclipse
entry.content:

entry.title: Joule Setup / Error during Booster Execution
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
we have tried to integrate Joule with our TDD System using the End-to-End Setup Guide (Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Bu... - SAP Community). For context the joule entitlement is in one subaccount and the workzone entitlements are in a different subaccount but under the same global account. Everything else was setup identical to the guide.

During the execution of the booster we encountered the following error:


or as text:
[SaaS Subscription/(module.joule_subscription.cis_saas_subscription.saas_subscription[0])] The subscription of subaccount (name of subaccount) to application das-application and plan foundation, failed with state: SUBSCRIBE_FAILED. - Error Message: 'Async callback of reuse service  'Document Grounding'  with appName document-grounding failed. It failed its asynchronous callback and responded with message: Service Document Grounding with appName document-grounding did not send its async callback response within the maximum allowed timeout of 300000 milliseconds. To resolve the issue, please contact this service's owner. (Root Application : Joule appName: das-application-ias. Please open support case using the component: CA-JOULE.)'. App Error: ''. 

Can anyone point us to what the issue might be and how to resolve it ?

Best regards 
Lukas

entry.title: Joule Capability Questions
entry.content:
I am working with a client who is evaluating SAP Joule Private Edition and would like to confirm whether it supports specific query and analysis capabilities across different SAP modules. Could you help clarify whether Joule can handle the following use cases?

1. Sales & Distribution (SD) Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Delivery Note (DN) details?

  • Can Joule query Sales Orders (SO) from a Customer's perspective? (For example: Tell me the due deliver sales order for customer ABCDE)

2. Materials Management (MM) Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Goods Receipt (GR) data?

  • Can Joule query Purchasing Orders (PO) from a Vendor's perspective? (For example: Tell me the due deliver purchasing order for vendor ABCDE)

3. Production Planning (PP) Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Goods Issue (GI) data for a Manufacturing Order (MO)?

  • Can Joule generate a Manufacturing Order (MO) Shortage Report?

4. Finance & Controlling (FICO) Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Overdue Accounts Receivable (AR)?

  • Can Joule convert data dimensions and aggregate (e.g., Customer → Customer Group → BUBG)(For example: Tell me the overdue AR percentage of a customer / customer group / BU)

  • Can Joule retrieve Credit Exposure details, specifically Credit Line Balance?

  • Can Joule retrieve Overdue Accounts Payable (AP) for Vendors?

  • Can Joule generate a Margin Report?

5. Business Partner Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Payment Terms?

  • Can Joule retrieve Incoterms?

Joule

entry.title: Identity Authentication Activation in Build Work Zone for SAP Joule (SUFA)
entry.content:
Hi together,
to activate SAP Joule in SuccessFactors (SUFA) as described in the Discovery Center mission, I need to enable Identity Authentication in SAP Build Work Zone. Unfortunately, I'm currently blocked due to access and configuration issues.

Here’s what has happened so far:

  • The SuccessFactors instance is successfully connected in a separate BTP Global Account.

  • I’ve subscribed to SAP Build Work Zone in my BTP Subaccount.

  • The trust to the IAS tenant is set up and active.

  • I manually created my user in IAS (with the same email address), but in the BTP cockpit, a second user appears – still without access to the “Identity Authentication” tab in Work Zone.

  • An admin account with access to the tenant also doesn’t see the necessary tab.

  • “Create Shadow Users During Logon” is enabled in the trust configuration.

I suspect that either the original service subscriber is not correctly linked in IAS, or there’s a mapping issue between users and the IAS setup.

Here’s the mission I’m working on:
https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/protected/index.html#/mymissiondetail/152454/card/11573701/?tab=projectboard

I’d appreciate support or a recommended workaround to proceed with the activation.

Thanks and best regards,
Lucas Maier
